explanation: The Sigma rule specifically looks for a literal space directly followed by the hyphens (' --remote-debugging-'). By wrapping the flag in double quotes, the parsed command line sequence becomes space-quote-hyphen-hyphen. This evades the exact substring match of the rule while still being interpreted correctly by the Chromium-based browser.

The Firefox selection strictly relies on the Image field ending exactly with '\firefox.exe'. By copying or renaming the executable to a different name (e.g., browser.exe) before execution, the Image condition fails to trigger, bypassing the rule entirely regardless of the command line arguments provided.

The rule expects a literal whitespace character (space) before the argument. By using a tab character (\t) as the argument separator instead of a standard space, the exact string match ' --remote-debugging-' fails, but the Windows command line will still parse and execute the argument normally.
